title: Race: requestNotificationPermission fire-and-forget may not complete before startService
description: background_service.dart calls _requestNotificationPermission() and awaits it, but on the Kotlin side MainActivity returns immediately (fire-and-forget). So Flutter resumes and calls startService before the system permission dialog has even been shown, let alone answered. On Android 13+, if the permission is not yet granted when startForeground() runs, a SecurityException is thrown. This is a race between the async permission flow and the sync service start. Fix: have MainActivity actually await the permission result and return the grant status to Dart before Dart calls startService.
close_reason: Kotlin now stores MethodChannel.Result and resolves it in onRequestPermissionsResult; Dart awaits before calling startService
